prerequisites: A Braintree gateway account. You can sign up for a sandbox account to test before using production.
Access to the Braintree Control Panel to retrieve your API credentials.
Your gateway Merchant ID, which is the unique identifier for your entire gateway account (found in the Control Panel under the gear icon > Business).
An API key pair (public key and private key) generated in the Control Panel under the gear icon > API > API Keys.
Knowledge of which environment to target: Sandbox (testing) or Production (live), since each environment has its own separate set of API keys.

authMethods.name: API key authentication (public key + private key)
authMethods.summary: Braintree authenticates server-side API requests with a public_key and private_key, scoped to a specific gateway via the merchant_id and to a specific environment (sandbox or production). The connector requires merchant_id, public_key, environment, and the secret private_key. The private key must be kept confidential.

setupSteps.title: Choose your environment
setupSteps.body: Decide whether to connect to Sandbox (for testing) or Production (for live data) and set the environment config field accordingly. API keys differ between the two environments, so credentials must match the selected environment.

setupSteps.title: Locate your Merchant ID
setupSteps.body: Log in to the Braintree Control Panel, click the gear icon, and open the Business menu at the top of the page to find your gateway Merchant ID. Enter this value in the merchant_id config field. Note this is the gateway identifier, not a merchant account ID.

setupSteps.title: Generate or retrieve your API key pair
setupSteps.body: In the Control Panel, click the gear icon, select API, and scroll to the API Keys section. Copy your Public Key into the public_key config field. In the Private Key column, click View to reveal the Private Key and enter it into the private_key secret field.

setupSteps.title: Set an optional start date
setupSteps.body: If desired, provide the optional start_date config field to bound how far back the source reads data. If omitted, the connector uses its default behavior.
